title: Chapter Forty-Six: The Golden Thread
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The crown prince walked away without looking back.

"Let's meet often," was all he had said. The way a man says *don't die* without saying the words.

Yeon-shin raised his head after bowing. The armory door had already closed. He stood in the fading light with Northern Light at his hip and the Desolate Sword beside it -- two blades now, the weight of them settling against his thigh like a promise.

Cheong Myeong appeared at his shoulder, arm draped across him with the easy familiarity of a man who had never once been refused anything in his life.

"That's how goodwill works," the Elf said. "Cannot be gained at once. Even goodwill is not enough if you want the Divine Sword Squad Leader's seat. Consistency. That is the answer."

"Accumulating merit comes first."

"Always so pleasant to talk to."

Cheong Myeong peeled away and left him standing in the twilight. Blue Eyes Demon Sword. Behind the friendliness there was a man who held nothing close -- not because he was cold, but because he had never needed to. Born into the world with talent and beauty and the serene detachment of the Elf clans.

Yeon-shin envied that. Just a little.

*Does it mean don't die elsewhere? Or don't die at all?*

He sheathed Northern Light. Its coolness ran up through the scabbard and into his palm -- a clean, sharp cold, like mountain water. Different from the Desolate Sword's warmth. Two swords now. Swords were consumables, they said. Blades that never dulled were rare treasures under heaven.

"Go ahead first," Yeon-shin said. "I have somewhere to be."

"Alright."

Cheong Myeong answered simply and turned. That was the Elf's way. Seemingly warm, but without attachment. No regret in anything.

Yeon-shin watched him go, then walked toward the Sect Leader's fortress.

---

The stone door opened before he reached it.

No hand touched it. No mechanism. The same as during the Desolate Exam -- the door simply moved, as if the fortress itself answered to the woman who lived inside.

Void Grasp. The realm of touching all things without touching them. A fragment of the martial arts that had allegedly destroyed the Dali Kingdom's royal family.

He stepped through. The corridor was wide and quiet. Torchlight played on stone walls carved with characters he could not read -- old script, from before the founding of Ming. The age of this place pressed against him. How many warriors had walked this path before him, seeking what he sought?

*Still need to increase internal energy quantity. If I could reach Void Grasp -- two swords floating in the air while I deploy Eternal Blossom Fist -- the effectiveness would be tremendous.*

A distant matter. But the image burned bright.

The thought had barely formed when a clear voice cut it short.

"You have gained leisure. Having other thoughts before me."

He looked up.

The tree trunks piercing through the office were strange no matter how many times he saw them. And on one of them, reclining as if she had grown there -- the Desolate Sect Lord.

Still the same presence. Beauty that defied years, looking only three or four summers older than himself. Deep green pupils that could stiffen souls. Her long pale green hair hung motionless, perfectly still.

*She contains her energy waves completely. What realm could that be?*

"You seem to have made up your mind," she said.

"I request internal energy techniques from the Sect Leader. Martial arts with fast energy absorption."

He had come to collect on a promise. The reward granted through his maternal grandfather's old friendship with the Sect Leader -- given to him after Ma Se-in's reckless sparring request. He had delayed long enough.

"Internal energy techniques," she said, and smiled. "After polishing the vessel, it must be filled with water. Something comes to mind. Come here."

He approached. Stood before her.

"What are you doing?"

"You said to come."

"Closer."

Her lips curved. "This seems to be your first proper guidance in energy absorption. If training manuals were sufficient, there would be no reason for masters to exist."

She drew herself upright on the branch. Cross-legged. Her pale green martial clothes brushed against the bark with a sound like leaves.

"Sit before me. Show your back. I will guide your true energy."

He stepped back three paces instead.

Slowly, deliberately, he bowed. One bow. The custom of the Central Plains -- one does not bow more than twice to the living.

She did not stop him.

*Acknowledgement.*

He rose. Turned. Sat with his back to her.

"It is called Moon Spirit Harmony Technique," she said. "My unique martial art."

Her hand touched his back.

Warm. Warmer than he had expected. Apart from her appearance, which might have been a sister's, he wondered if this was how a mother's touch felt.

The thought lasted one heartbeat.

*Boom.*

Pure internal energy beyond anything he had imagined flooded through him. A clear stream of energy, as if nature itself had been distilled into liquid form. Every fine meridian, every energy channel in his body filled in sequence -- showing him the path.

"This is how it is done. Feel it."

He closed his eyes. Her voice guided the chants. Her energy showed the way. Gradually, his own intent began to circulate on its own.

"Yes. That is it."

Half an hour. That was all it took.

When her fingertips lifted from his back, the energy dispersed like morning mist. He had learned the divine technique in a single circulation. No more was needed.

A breath of laughter tickled the back of his neck.

"Come ask if there are blockages."

"Thank you for the reward."

He descended and bowed again with clasped fists. She smiled -- that same unknowable smile.

On the path back to the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion, his heart was light.

*Very good.*

The inspiration was surging. Moon Spirit Harmony Technique did not reject his Jeong Family Dynamic Technique. Like her energy -- embracing all things.

He could create internal energy techniques far surpassing third-rate. He was certain of it.

*I want to see Se-in.*

The thought surprised him. Ma Se-in, his distant cousin. And his inadequate maternal grandfather, Ma Yeon-jeok. Without their meddling -- without that reckless sparring request -- this reward would never have existed.

Strange, what gratitude could survive even between broken families.

He walked on. The evening air was sweet with early blossoms, and the fortress lights were coming on one by one across Desolate City, warm and amber against the deepening blue.

---

Yeon-shin devoted himself to internal energy cultivation and creation until the next evening. It was the first time since childhood that he had sat still, cross-legged beneath his bed platform, refining internal force. Focusing on the living, breathing energy inside and outside his body. Drawing it in with Moon Spirit Harmony Technique's pathways. Releasing it. Drawing it in again, each time a little more, a little deeper.

One full major heavenly circuit of full body circulation. Then another.

When Do Yu-won from the General Administration Office came to his room the next evening, he carried two sets of blue clothes on his arm, and a smile that split his face.

"Truly congratulatory," the administrative officer said, beaming. "Another direct disciple of the Sect Leader has been born."

Yeon-shin looked at what he carried.

Blue martial uniform. Nearly identical to the one he had received at promotion. But the character embroidered on both shoulders and back -- the rough *Hwang* that marked all of Desolate Sect -- was stitched in golden thread.

Gold.

"Those who receive the Sect Leader's martial arts instruction carry symbolism regardless of rank," Do Yu-won explained. "They are bound to become famous. The General Administration Office prepared this for the ceremony."

"Thank you."

"Also -- we watched His Highness the crown prince, who already receives instruction. Though the Sect Leader does not mind, it is different for us. But for some reason, they say he let it pass smoothly."

Meaning the crown prince had not protested sharing his master.

*Interesting.*

Yeon-shin calmly nodded and saw Do Yu-won off. He changed immediately. The fabric felt the same against his skin -- the same Heaven Silkworm weave -- but the golden characters changed everything. He could feel their weight without touching them.

He left. Ma Jin had summoned first team.

The golden characters caught every eye on the walk to the training ground. Seniors he passed widened their eyes. A few stopped mid-stride. He brushed off their stares with the indifference of a man who had simply put on a different coat, but he noticed them all the same.

At the main training ground, Radiant Demon First Team had assembled. Ma Jin. Hyeon Won-chang. And the junior newly assigned to the team -- Shin So-bin.

"S-sir! What is that glorious golden appearance?!"

Hyeon Won-chang's fuss was loud and theatrical. Yeon-shin endured it.

Ma Jin spoke.

"Under blue-level Lightning Flash's leadership, Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin will set out on the mission. Young faces are needed."

"Young faces meaning?"

"There are people called a righteous martial family. The Hwangbo family." Ma Jin twisted the scar at his mouth corner. "The truth is different. Azure Sky Squad uncovered their secret long ago. They are forcing commoners to build their new base without payment. Forced labor -- not ordered by the imperial court. Over a thousand people."

"Despicable!" Hyeon Won-chang spat.

"Common," Shin So-bin said.

The contrast hung in the air for a moment.

"They dominate Jinan territory in Shandong," Ma Jin continued. "Already acting like royalty. There is a limit to exposing their behavior publicly. Lightning Flash. You are important."

"What is the mission?"

"Hide your identity. Participate in the Dragon Phoenix Gathering with two white-levels. It happens to be held in South Zhili under the Namgung family's supervision. Since Shandong is right above it, the Hwangbo young master will attend."

Ma Jin paused. The scar at his mouth corner deepened. When he spoke again, his voice had changed -- not the uncle's voice, but the Radiant Demon Wing Leader's.

"Your mission is provocation. Scratch his nerves. Make him go mad. Make it so he mobilizes his family to eliminate you. Their power must move greatly -- so gaps show in their hold over Jinan."

"What happens if I succeed?"

"While you are in South Zhili, we extract witnesses and evidence. Three of the Seventeen Squads will gather. All of Radiant Demon Wing with Azure Sky Squad and Annihilation Team strike the Hwangbo family together."

*Annihilation of one of the Eight Great Families.*

Three Divine Sword Squads converging. Invincible.

"About the young master," Yeon-shin said, keeping his voice level. "If the situation is unfavorable -- can I take his head?"

"That is left to a blue warrior's discretion. That is what blue-level rank means."

Ma Jin smiled. Beside him, Baek Mi-ryeo's expression was troubled.

"Be aware this is very dangerous," she said. "Don't overdo it. That young master is fated to die anyway. Born with a short-lived constitution in exchange for tremendous talent."

Cheong Myeong added a word from behind, his voice carrying its usual lightness.

"They say he is a genius of the Solar Divine Meridian. He will be quite strong."

*Solar Divine Meridian.*

Yeon-shin let the words settle.

A genius born to die young. Tremendous talent bought with a shortened life.

The door inside him rattled. He kept it shut.

---
